results.response_text: Fixed. The duplicated `redactSecretDiagnostics` secret-redaction helper (previously copied verbatim into three github-script steps: "Post OCR results", "Redact OCR diagnostic artifacts", and the notify-ocr-infrastructure-failure job) has been extracted into a single checked-in shared module at `.github/scripts/ocr-redaction.js`. Each step now `require()`s that module from the trusted `GITHUB_WORKSPACE` base-branch checkout and keeps only a thin per-step wrapper that binds the step-specific secret values (endpoint/token). This makes the redaction patterns the single source of truth, so any new secret format is added in exactly one place and can no longer drift across steps. Fork-safety is preserved because `pull_request_target` runs the base-branch workflow and the module is only ever loaded from the trusted base checkout (never PR-supplied code); the notify job now performs a trusted base-branch checkout before downloading artifacts so the shared module is available. Verified with `node --check` on the module, a functional redaction smoke test, and a YAML parse of the workflow. See commit 6aa8d6fe96052c86d11acc36cd207faeebb4e169.

evidence.summary: Extracted the duplicated redactSecretDiagnostics helper from three inline github-script copies into a single shared module (.github/scripts/ocr-redaction.js). All three OCR workflow steps now require() the shared module and retain only a thin wrapper binding step-specific secrets. No inline redaction pattern chains remain in the workflow.

commands.description: Node.js syntax check of the shared redaction module.

argv: const {redactSecretDiagnostics}=require('./.github/scripts/ocr-redaction.js'); const out=redactSecretDiagnostics('Authorization: Bearer abc token=ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOP1234 secret_val', ['abc']); if(!out.includes('[REDACTED]'))process.exit(1)
commands.description: Functional smoke test confirming the shared module redacts secrets.
YAML parse validation of the OCR PR review workflow.
Confirms zero inline redaction pattern chains remain in the workflow (all patterns now live only in the shared module).
